info.name: ValidatorForge API
info.description: GraphQL operations control plane for ValidatorForge — a Solana validator-fleet ops node. Register nodes, launch deployment sagas (provision / zero-downtime upgrade / hot-spare failover / decommission), inspect the type-state node lifecycle and saga run history, ask the AI advisor for a recommendation, and stream live ops events over a GraphQL subscription. Set the `baseUrl` collection variable (default http://localhost:8080). Start the server with `validatorforge-node serve`. Deployments are admission-gated by a token-bucket rate limiter and a bounded bulkhead (errors with code `throttled`); every saga effect inherits timeout + retry + circuit-breaker.

request.description: Register (upsert) a node. It starts in the `provisioning` state of the type-state lifecycle. Invalid ids/hosts/versions are rejected at the domain boundary (e.g. `empty_field`, `too_long`).

Run a provision saga: apply_infra → tune_host → start_validator → await_catchup. Returns the terminal run record (status `succeeded` / `rolled_back` / `failed`). On success the node walks bootstrapping → catching_up → active.
Zero-downtime upgrade saga: drain → start_validator → await_catchup. `targetVersion` is required for `UPGRADE` (omitting it errors).
Hot-spare failover saga: drain → swap_identity (compensation swaps identity back). `spare` is required for `FAILOVER` and must be a registered node.
Service health + number of tracked nodes.
Fetch one node by id (null when unknown).
Fetch one run by id (null when unknown).

Ask the advisor agent for a JSON recommendation { action, urgency, rationale }. Backed by the deterministic HeuristicAdvisor by default; an LlmAdvisor (feature `llm`) falls back to the heuristic on any provider error.
